# DUST-20 | Bitcoin Universe Protocol Reference

> Human docs: index.html
> Field reference: reference.html
> Network: Bitcoin
> Classification: Dyed dust UTXO fungibility experiment

## Summary

A Bitcoin-native fungibility experiment where units are represented by dyed satoshis in UTXOs, so a transfer can be a normal Bitcoin split rather than a new transfer inscription.

## State model

Deploy and mint messages establish a color rule. Indexers aggregate matching UTXOs, normally at 546 sats per unit.

## Lifecycle

1. Deploy rule
2. Mint colored UTXO
3. Split outputs
4. Aggregate balance

## Operations

- Deploy: Sets supply, sats per unit, total sat backing, and an optional per-mint sat cap.
- Mint: Creates a colored UTXO whose value must match the declared sats.
- Transfer: Splits or combines colored UTXOs. Fees should come from a separate non-colored input.

## Safety

1. The mint output value must equal the sats declared in the message. A mismatch breaks the colored-UTXO rule.
2. Keep miner-fee inputs separate from colored UTXOs so a fee cannot consume asset-bearing sats.
3. Use a wallet and indexer that understand DUST-20 selection, splitting, and change behavior.

## Sources

- Legacy DUST-20 documentation: https://dust-20.gitbook.io/dust20
- Universe Inscribe: https://inscribe.bitcoinuniverse.io
